summary: Rebuilt the visible prototype flow in clean Korean. Google OAuth now leads into group creation, invite links can be accepted at /join/[code], Kakao map has visible fallback diagnostics, place search has REST fallback, AI is segmented into course/wishlist/link flows, and wishlist completion can publish a map pin locally.
completed_steps: Added /api/groups/ensure, /api/groups/join, and /join/[code] for real Supabase-backed invite acceptance.
Connected login mode selection to /api/groups/ensure for Google users.
Rebuilt app home, sidebar, map, wishlist, and AI pages with clean Korean copy.
Added /api/kakao/search using KAKAO_REST_API_KEY as fallback when Kakao JS SDK services are not available.
Guarded /api/capture and capture cache so prototype mode and missing optional envs do not crash the route.
Kept Gemini calls centralized on gemini-3.0-flash only.
next_steps: Apply schema.sql to Supabase if not already applied; groups/group_members/users are required for invite APIs.
Implement Supabase-backed wishlist/community persistence so partners and friends see shared pins across devices.
Smoke-test deployed Vercel domain in Kakao Developers JavaScript SDK domain settings and Supabase OAuth redirect URL settings.
If Gemini rejects gemini-3.0-flash, confirm the exact model id enabled in the user's Gemini project and update lib/ai/model.ts only if the user approves.
blockers: Cross-device shared pins are not fully persisted yet; current map share is local prototype storage.
gemini-3.0-flash model availability depends on the user's Gemini API account/project.
